summary: I grew up in Tijuana, Mexico, in a big family but a small house. It was always noisy with people coming and going, and though we didn't have much money, it was home. Later, I fulfilled my dream by opening my own horse track right there in Tijuana, where I trained and sold horses. Unfortunately, the track burned down due to an employee's carelessness, which forced me to leave Tijuana and start over elsewhere.

Mexico is where my roots are; I was born and raised there, specifically in Tijuana. It was in Mexico that I developed my passion for horses and even opened my own horse track. After the devastating fire that destroyed my business, my wife and I made the difficult decision to leave Mexico and seek a new life in California. Despite moving away, Mexico will always be my home and an important part of my identity.

After losing everything in Mexico, my wife and I moved to California to live with her family, arriving with nothing but the clothes on our backs. Starting over was scary, but I kept my passion alive by training horses again in California. Over time, I rebuilt my career, became a citizen, and raised my family here. California became my second home, and I am proud of the life we created in this state.

We settled in Alameda County, California, where we bought a house and continued our lives happily. It was here that our baby girl, Sunday, was born, and I was able to rebuild my horse training career. Living in Alameda County gave me a sense of stability and success after the hardships we faced. This place truly became the foundation for our new life in the United States.
